pr.title: Use portable bash shebang for asmdiff
pr.author: r-burns

summary: Updated the repo-root asmdiff helper script to use a portable Bash shebang, replacing hardcoded /bin/bash with /usr/bin/env bash. The PR body states this matters because some systems do not install bash at /bin/bash. The diff also leaves the final diff command content unchanged while adding a trailing newline at end of file.

what_changed.change: Changed the script shebang from #!/bin/bash to #!/usr/bin/env bash.
Preserved the objdump-based comparison flow using DEVKITPPC powerpc-eabi-objdump, baserom.dol, build/ssbm.us.1.2/main.dol, and diff -u --color=always.
Added a newline at end of file, as shown by the removal of the previous no-newline marker.

smart_moves: Used /usr/bin/env bash instead of an absolute /bin/bash path, allowing the user's PATH to locate bash on systems where it is installed elsewhere.
Kept the change minimal: only the shebang and EOF newline changed, reducing risk to the asmdiff behavior used for matching work.
decomp_lessons: Repository helper scripts used in matching workflows should avoid host-specific assumptions such as bash living at /bin/bash.
Small portability fixes to tooling can reduce friction for contributors across Linux, macOS, and other Unix-like environments without affecting decomp output.
When modifying scripts like asmdiff.sh, preserve the exact objdump and diff behavior unless the PR explicitly intends to change comparison semantics.
naming_conventions: No symbol, function, struct, or header naming conventions were introduced or discussed in this PR.
The existing script variables OBJDUMP and OPTIONS remained uppercase shell variables.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: asmdiff.sh compares disassembly ranges from baserom.dol and build/ssbm.us.1.2/main.dol using powerpc-eabi-objdump with Gekko/PowerPC options.
The PR did not change the matching tactic itself; it only made the Bash interpreter lookup portable.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Find other repository shell scripts with hardcoded /bin/bash shebangs.
Check whether asmdiff.sh is still portable across contributor environments using DEVKITPPC.
Search for later changes to asmdiff.sh that alter objdump options or diff behavior.
